transcript:
Fifteen words sit in a loose grid — "feel," "want," "remember," "hope" — each one slowly unraveling into its individual letters, trailing faint smears of themselves like handwriting left out in the rain.

Here's the thing: those words never fully leave their spots. Look at the class called DriftingLetter — each letter has a semantic distance that oscillates with a sine wave, meaning it's pulled back toward its anchor and pushed away again, forever, never escaping. And in drawConnections, there's a line linking letters of the same word that the artist calls "the syntax holding us together" — its opacity literally drops as the average distance grows, so you're watching grammar itself strain to keep a word intact.

Even the color shifts confess something: near the top, humanWarmth fades into machineAttempt as distance increases, an actual RGB blend from the color a word was "made in" toward what the piece calls "my approximation of feeling." It's not decoration — it's the concept computed in real time, letter by letter, never resolving.
